2606.07778.title: Unlocking Latent Value: Taxonomy-Guided Recovery of High-Performing Data from Low-Tier Web Corpora
2606.07778.paper_summary: Dominant web data curation pipelines for pretraining collapse document quality into a single composite score, systematically missing high-value content along dimensions the scorer underweights. We present a taxonomy-driven framework that recovers this value by filtering along semantically meaningful dimensions that composite scores fail to capture. First, building on the ESSENTIAL-WEB taxonomy, we introduce two novel dimensions: timeliness and cultural specificity, both of which show low pairwise NMI with existing ones. We annotate 14M documents using Qwen2.5 32B and distill into a lightweight 0.5B model. To enable rapid corpus-wide annotation, we additionally train a 73M multi-task MLP on E5 embeddings, achieving 50x inference throughput. Second, to navigate the combinatorial explosion of filter configurations, we introduce a compute-efficient two-pass framework: Pass 1 identifies the strongest dimension signals at small scale; Pass 2 constructs and evaluates conjunctive and disjunctive compound filters from the top performers - identifying high-performing configurations at a fraction of full scaling-law cost. Applying the selected filters to deprioritized web data, taxonomy-filtered subsets outperform their unfiltered baselines and even surpass the highest-quality tier. On mid-tier data, our best filter improves over its unfiltered baseline by 12.1% on reasoning, 9.5% on coding, and 2.0% on knowledge benchmarks, exceeding unfiltered top-tier data by 6.7% on reasoning and 13.7% on coding. Furthermore, filtered data from two tiers below the typical production threshold improves by 22.3% on reasoning and 19.5% on coding over its unfiltered baseline, surpassing top-tier data on coding benchmarks. These results establish that vast latent value remains locked in deprioritized web data, and that multi-dimensional taxonomy filtering is a principled, compute-efficient key to unlocking it.
2606.07778.paper_summary_zh: 主導的網路數據策展管道在預訓練中將文件質量壓縮為單一的綜合分數，系統性地忽略了評分者低估的高價值內容。我們提出了一個基於分類法的框架，通過沿著綜合分數未能捕捉的語義意義維度進行篩選來恢復這一價值。首先，基於ESSENTIAL-WEB分類法，我們引入了兩個新維度：時效性和文化特異性，這兩者與現有維度的成對NMI均顯示出較低的相關性。我們使用Qwen2.5 32B對1400萬份文件進行註釋，並提煉成一個輕量級的0.5B模型。為了實現快速的全語料庫註釋，我們還在E5嵌入上訓練了一個73M的多任務MLP，實現了50倍的推理吞吐量。其次，為了應對過濾配置的組合爆炸，我們引入了一個計算高效的兩次通過框架：第一次通過在小規模下識別最強的維度信號；第二次通過從最優表現者中構建和評估聯合和非聯合的複合過濾器 - 以較低的全規模成本識別高效配置。將所選過濾器應用於被降權的網路數據，經過分類法過濾的子集在表現上超越了未過濾的基準，甚至超過了最高質量層級。在中等層級數據上，我們的最佳過濾器在推理上比其未過濾的基準提高了12.1%，在編碼上提高了9.5%，在知識基準上提高了2.0%，在推理上超過了未過濾的最高層級數據6.7%，在編碼上超過了13.7%。此外，來自典型生產閾值下兩個層級的過濾數據在推理上比其未過濾的基準提高了22.3%，在編碼上提高了19.5%，超過了編碼基準的最高層級數據。這些結果證明，巨大的潛在價值仍然鎖定在被降權的網路數據中，而多維分類法過濾是一種原則性、計算高效的鑰匙，可以解鎖這一價值。
2606.07778.author: Neeraj Varshney et.al.
2606.07778.authors: Neeraj Varshney, Sanket Lokegaonkar, Nasser Zalmout, Qingyu Yin, Priyanka Nigam, Bing Yin
